north_star: Connect a GitHub repo, translate it into a playable city, and fight AI bots attacking the project through coding battles.
guiding_rules: Do not treat the repo as a literal city; build a translation layer.
Metadata-first and read-only GitHub integration in the MVP.
Keep the current mission/district abstractions if they still help.
Prefer bounded slices over giant rewrites.
Preserve app runnability after every slice.
Prioritize clarity, trust, and smoothness over spectacle.
After Phase 0, docs-only cycles do not count as progress.
After Phase 0, every cycle must change code and run validation.

milestones.name: Phase 1 - Repo Model Foundation
deliverables: Repo-to-city generator with bridge to game types (shared/repoCityGenerator.ts)
Frontend repo selector in MainMenu with loadRepoCity in gameStore
Worker endpoints for fixture listing and city generation

validation.browser_smoke: not_run — MainMenu enter-city testid preserved, selector is additive

milestones.name: Phase 2 - Static Repo City Generation
deliverables: Repo archetype classifier (heuristic rules based on module kinds and languages)
District generation rules (improve layout, naming, size/heat tuning)
Road/dependency mapping rules (visual connectivity from DependencyEdge data)

completed_slices.title: Dependency-edge road mapping and repo-city road rendering
summary: GeneratedCity now includes readable roads derived from the strongest dependency edges.
Repo-city mode renders generated roads in the 3D scene while classic mode keeps the legacy street grid.
Repo city summaries now expose road counts to make the payload visible from the menu.

completed_slices.title: Archetype-aware district naming and layout templates
summary: The generator now derives an archetype from repo structure when laying out the city.
District labels are stylized from module path hints so fullstack and library repos read differently.
Support modules pull toward central slots while app and service districts stay on clearer perimeter positions.

validation.generator_dry_run: pass — fullstack and library fixtures now produce distinct labels and placements
validation.frontend_lint: not_run — no frontend file changed in this slice
validation.browser_smoke: not_run — no UI flow or selector changes

completed_slices.title: Backend repo fixture and classifier coverage
summary: Added a seeded backend repo fixture that flows through the shared fixture list, worker fixture endpoint, and frontend repo selector.
Generator dry runs now cover fullstack, library, and backend repos with classifier output matching each seeded archetype.
Backend fixture output produces a distinct city shape with runtime, data, validation, and control districts.

validation.generator_dry_run: pass — fixture and generated archetypes match for fullstack, library, and backend repos
validation.frontend_lint: not_run — no frontend file changed in this slice
validation.browser_smoke: not_run — no UI flow or selector changes

completed_slices.title: Monorepo fixture and workspace heuristics
summary: Added a seeded monorepo repo fixture with apps, packages, services, tests, and workspace control metadata.
Tightened monorepo classification around workspace-style roots and config hints instead of treating any single services folder as a monorepo.
Monorepo labels and layout now emphasize workspace cores and control near the hub while app districts stay on the perimeter.

validation.generator_dry_run: pass — fixture and generated archetypes match for fullstack, library, backend, and monorepo repos
validation.frontend_lint: not_run — no frontend file changed in this slice
validation.browser_smoke: not_run — no UI flow or selector changes

completed_slices.title: Frontend-only fixture and language-share tie breakers
summary: Added a seeded frontend-only repo fixture with UI, styles, assets, storybook, tests, and CI metadata.
Classifier heuristics now use frontend language share plus surface-folder hints to resolve ambiguous UI repos before falling back to library or unknown.
Generator dry runs now cover fullstack, library, backend, frontend, and monorepo repos with classifier output matching each seeded archetype.

validation.generator_dry_run: pass — fixture and generated archetypes match for fullstack, library, backend, frontend, and monorepo repos
validation.frontend_lint: not_run — no frontend file changed in this slice
validation.browser_smoke: not_run — no UI flow or selector changes

completed_slices.title: Minimal repo-city surface overlay
summary: Added a new SVG-based repo-city surface in play mode that renders districts, routes, the player marker, and the active waypoint from GeneratedCity data.
Repo-city mode now sidelines the old district blocks and hologram props so the new surface becomes the primary readable map layer without touching mission or movement logic yet.
The existing menu-to-city flow and browser smoke path remain intact while repo-city mode now looks visibly distinct from classic mode.

completed_slices.title: Surface-native district entry and mission routing cues
summary: The repo-city surface now supports direct district entry in play mode by snapping the player to a district center when the surface is clicked outside of an active mission.

Available-mission districts now advertise themselves on the surface, and the map shows a live guidance line to hovered districts or active mission waypoints so entry and approach cues no longer depend on the hidden legacy meshes.
The local smoke bridge now reuses the same district-move helper as the UI so map-driven and scripted district entry stay consistent.

completed_slices.title: Repo-city minimalist HUD shell baseline
summary: Repo-city mode now uses a lighter status shell that keeps credits, reputation, repo identity, district state, and mission timing readable without leaning on the old heavy chrome.
The duplicated legacy minimap and direction arrow are now removed from repo-city mode because the SVG surface already carries map position and routing cues.
Mission route and capture overlays now restyle into compact repo-city cards so the active play screen feels more consistent even while the legacy mission panel remains in place.

completed_slices.title: Repo-city mission panel shell alignment
summary: The mission panel now adopts repo-city-specific header, summary, and card styling so mission browsing matches the calmer map-first shell instead of the older neon-heavy side panel.
Mission cards now surface route type and district context directly in repo-city mode while keeping the existing accept flow and smoke selectors intact.
Empty and write-session states now inherit the same repo-city panel language, reducing the remaining visual break between the map surface and mission selection.

completed_slices.title: Repo-city leaderboard and bulletin overlay alignment
summary: The optional leaderboard and bulletin overlays now adopt repo-city-specific headers, close controls, and calmer card styling so they read as part of the same minimalist shell.
Repo-city leaderboard entries now surface district-control and mission-clear metrics without falling back to the older arcade-style chrome.
Repo-city bulletin items now expose district context and severity chips in a compact feed layout while preserving the existing overlay toggles and smoke flow.

completed_slices.title: Repo-city connection banner and reward toast shell alignment
summary: The worker status banner now uses repo-city-specific headers, state pills, and calmer action styling in repo-city mode while preserving the existing retry behavior and smoke selectors.
Reward toasts now render as compact repo-city shell cards with route-clear framing and structured credit and reputation metrics instead of the older arcade-style popup chrome.
Mobile repo-city layouts now reposition both transient surfaces to stay readable inside the minimalist shell without changing their underlying behavior.

completed_slices.title: Repo-city merge conflict boss shell alignment
summary: Repo-city boss encounters now render inside a calmer shell with route context, district and reward pills, and a structured timer block instead of dropping back to the older neon-heavy merge conflict overlay.
Conflict options now present as repo-city candidate cards with clearer side labels and calmer success and failure result states while keeping the existing resolution loop intact.
The boss overlay now includes repo-city mobile spacing and panel sizing so conflict encounters stay readable within the minimalist shell.

completed_slices.title: Repo-city selector panel shell alignment
summary: The repo selector now uses a repo-city-specific header, close control, and shell card treatment in repo-city mode while keeping the existing seeded fixture chooser behavior intact.
Repo fixtures now read as structured repo-city cards with branch, archetype, language, and repo-structure metrics instead of the older flat neon list rows.
The selector panel now includes repo-city mobile sizing and spacing while classic-mode selector content order and behavior remain unchanged.

completed_slices.title: Repo-city main menu action shell alignment
summary: The repo-city entry CTA now uses a calmer shell card with launch context and city-readiness metadata instead of the older neon-heavy start button chrome.
Repo-city secondary actions now render as structured action cards for changing repos or returning to classic mode while preserving the existing menu behavior and enter-city smoke selector.
The main menu action row now includes repo-city desktop and mobile sizing so the Phase 3 entry point feels consistent with the rest of the minimalist shell.

completed_slices.title: Repo-city menu hero and summary shell alignment
summary: The repo-city main menu title and subtitle now render inside a calmer hero shell with connected-repo context instead of relying on the older neon-heavy title chrome.
The repo summary now reads as a structured snapshot card with archetype framing and district, road, mission, and threat metrics while preserving the existing enter-city flow.
Desktop and mobile repo-city menu layouts now keep the header copy and summary metrics readable without changing selectors or selector-panel behavior.

completed_slices.title: Repo-city menu backdrop and footer shell alignment
summary: Repo-city mode now uses a calmer full-screen menu backdrop with subtle grid and gradient framing instead of the older stark radial menu treatment.
The floating version string now renders as a structured footer note with repo-city pill styling and snapshot context while preserving the existing menu flow and enter-city selector.
Desktop and mobile repo-city menu frame elements now read as part of the same minimalist shell without changing selector-panel behavior.

completed_slices.title: Repo-city scene ground and lighting tone alignment
summary: Repo-city play mode now uses a calmer canvas background, softer ground palette, and reduced fog and lighting contrast beneath the SVG surface instead of inheriting the older stark scene treatment.
Generated repo roads now render with more subdued materials, and repos without generated roads fall back to muted guide lanes instead of the legacy neon street grid.
Classic mode keeps its previous scene look while repo-city mode drops the old street-light spectacle so the minimalist surface remains the primary readable layer.

completed_slices.title: Repo-city marker tone alignment
summary: Repo-city play mode now uses calmer player, waypoint, and mission-trigger materials so the in-world markers stop competing with the SVG surface.
Waypoint labels now render as quieter shell-like callouts in repo-city mode while routing, trigger opening, and waypoint reach behavior remain unchanged.
Classic mode keeps the previous bright marker treatment while repo-city mode drops the older neon-heavy avatar and beam language.

completed_slices.title: Repo-city camera framing alignment
summary: Repo-city play mode now uses a higher, steadier follow camera and a narrower field of view so the in-world scene better matches the top-down SVG surface.
The initial canvas camera and runtime follow behavior now stay aligned in repo-city mode while movement, mission routing, and district entry remain unchanged.
Classic mode keeps the previous chase-style framing while repo-city drops the older shared camera relationship.

completed_slices.title: Repo-city movement feel alignment
summary: Repo-city play mode now uses a slower, damped locomotion profile with softer sprinting so keyboard traversal feels more deliberate from the top-down surface view.
External district moves now clear local movement momentum so surface-driven entry, waypoint routing, and mission triggers stay stable after the movement adjustment.
Classic mode keeps the previous immediate locomotion behavior while repo-city drops the older arcade-fast shared movement feel.

completed_slices.title: Repo-city surface transit replaces district snap
summary: Repo-city surface clicks now queue a short readable transit toward the selected district instead of teleporting the player instantly, while classic-mode scripted district moves keep their previous immediate behavior.
The player now physically traverses into the destination district before district state flips, so mission trigger opening and arrival cues stay tied to actual entry rather than a premature snap.
The surface keeps the queued district highlighted during transit and closes stale mission-panel context until the player reaches the new district.

completed_slices.title: Repo-city surface transit follows generated roads
summary: Repo-city transit now prefers a bounded road-guided path through generated district connections instead of cutting straight across the map whenever a short readable route exists.
The player steps through routed transit points and the surface guidance overlay now mirrors the routed path, so the visible cue and in-world movement stay aligned during transit.
Intermediate district trigger rings no longer pop the mission panel during a pass-through, while the target district still keeps its normal arrival behavior.

completed_slices.title: Repo-city 3D scene highlights active transit roads
summary: Repo-city transit now carries the ids of the generated roads selected for the active routed path, without changing direct transit or classic-mode behavior.
The 3D scene now brightens the active routed road segments and dashes during transit so the in-world scene visibly matches the road-guided surface path.
The highlight clears automatically when routed transit ends or is canceled, keeping the calmer default road tone for idle repo-city exploration.

completed_slices.title: Repo-city HUD shell exposes active transit status
summary: Repo-city HUD now shows a dedicated transit card while transit is active, including the destination district, remaining distance, and whether the route is direct or road-guided.
The transit card is repo-city-only and clears automatically on arrival or cancel because it is derived from the existing transit state instead of introducing a parallel HUD flag.
The new card keeps transit context readable from the shell even when the player is not watching the surface or 3D road highlight directly.

completed_slices.title: Repo-city mission panel follows queued transit district
summary: Repo-city mission browsing now scopes available routes to the queued transit destination while transit is active instead of staying pinned to the current district until arrival.
Mission-panel header, subtitle, and empty-state copy now explicitly frame the queued district as the active route preview so surface, HUD, and panel context stay aligned during approach.
Classic mode and non-transit repo-city browsing keep their previous current-district or city-wide scope without introducing new mission state.

completed_slices.title: Repo-city district status shell follows queued transit district
summary: Repo-city HUD district status now scopes its title, heat pill, presence, and stability chips to the queued transit destination while transit is active instead of staying pinned to the source district.
The repo-city surface legend now switches its district-status row and heat pill to the queued destination during transit, while canceled or idle transit falls back to current-district or idle copy.
Classic mode and repo-city movement behavior stay unchanged because the slice only re-scopes display copy and shell summaries around the existing transit state.

completed_slices.title: Repo-city objective tracker aligns with the route-first shell
summary: Repo-city missions now render their tracker as an active-route shell with mission title, route type, target district, current cue, and step progress instead of the older generic objective checklist header.

Route steps now keep the existing mission progression logic but restyle into calmer repo-city cards with numbered progression and current-distance context while classic mode keeps the previous objective list unchanged.
The tracker now reads coherently beside the transit card, mission panel, and district-status shell without changing smoke selectors or underlying mission state.

completed_slices.title: Repo-city capture overlay aligns with the stability shell
summary: Repo-city district recovery now renders as a structured stability card with district title, state pill, progress chips, and calmer shell framing instead of the older centered capture overlay.
The stability progress bar and status copy now read as repo-city shell elements while keeping the existing capture progression and current-district behavior unchanged.
Classic mode keeps the previous territory-capture overlay and copy, so the slice stays isolated to repo-city presentation.

completed_slices.title: Repo-city mission-active bar aligns with the route shell
summary: Repo-city in-mission status now renders as an active-route shell with mission title, route type, district, step progress, and current cue instead of the older simple title-plus-objective strip.
The repo-city mission timer now sits inside the calmer route shell while keeping the existing timer countdown and waypoint behavior unchanged.
Classic mode keeps the previous mission-active bar and separate timer treatment, so the slice stays isolated to repo-city presentation.

completed_slices.title: Repo-city HUD control row aligns with shell action cards
summary: Repo-city bottom HUD controls now render as calmer action cards with primary labels and secondary shortcut or state copy instead of shared legacy button text.
Mission, ranking, and bulletin toggles keep their existing behavior and smoke selector coverage while the repo-city row now reads coherently with the rest of the shell.
Classic mode keeps the previous HUD button treatment, so the slice stays isolated to repo-city presentation.

completed_slices.title: Repo-city top stat block aligns with shell metrics
summary: Repo-city top HUD stats now render inside calmer shell metrics with a shared resources kicker, per-metric card framing, and secondary context copy instead of the older centered neon block.
Credits and reputation values keep their existing behavior while classic mode preserves the previous stat treatment, so the slice stays isolated to repo-city presentation.
Desktop and mobile repo-city layouts now keep the stat block aligned with the badge, transit card, and district shell without changing mission flow or smoke selectors.

completed_slices.title: Repo-city surface legend aligns with shell metrics
summary: The repo-city surface legend now renders as a calmer metric-card grid with a shared shell kicker instead of flatter utility rows in the corner of the map.

District status, surface focus, entry or approach cue, and heat state keep their existing labels and logic while the legend now exposes clearer secondary context copy without changing movement or district-selection behavior.
Desktop and mobile repo-city surface layouts now keep those metrics readable as part of the shell while classic mode and smoke selectors remain unchanged.

completed_slices.title: Repo-city surface header meta aligns with shell chips
summary: The repo-city surface header now renders archetype, district count, and route count inside structured shell chips instead of flatter utility spans beside the repo title.
The same repo identity and metric values remain exposed while the header meta now reads more coherently with the repo-city shell without changing surface behavior.
Desktop and mobile repo-city surface layouts now keep those chips readable and balanced beside the header title block while classic mode and smoke selectors remain unchanged.

completed_slices.title: Repo-city surface header title block aligns with shell copy
summary: The repo-city surface header title block now renders inside a calmer shell card instead of a flatter kicker-plus-title stack while preserving the same repo owner/name and surface role text.
The title block now uses clearer chip-style role framing and calmer copy treatment without changing repo-city surface behavior or selectors.
Desktop and mobile repo-city surface headers now keep the identity block balanced beside the metric chips while classic mode remains unchanged.

completed_slices.title: Align repo-city surface mission badges with shell chips
summary: The district mission count markers on the repo-city surface now render as rounded shell-style chips that match the calmer chip language of the header and legend instead of the previous yellow circular badge treatment.
Mission availability logic, district selection behavior, and active-mission suppression of the marker remain unchanged.
The change is isolated to RepoCitySurface.tsx and index.css; frontend build and lint pass cleanly.

completed_slices.title: Phase 3 wrap-up — final verification and phase close
summary: Added data-testid='phase-3-complete' to App.tsx root for selector-driven validation.
All 30 Phase 3 slices verified: city map, district view, movement layer, and minimalist UI shell deliverables complete.
Frontend build and lint pass. Phase 3 closed.

completed_slices.title: GitHub OAuth login — minimal token acquisition flow
summary: Added a frontend GitHub login entry in the main menu plus SPA callback handling that exchanges an OAuth code and stores the resulting access token in Zustand state.
Worker now exposes a minimal GitHub OAuth start redirect and token exchange endpoint so the frontend can complete the read-only OAuth handshake without adding repo selection yet.
The logged-in UI is intentionally minimal and only confirms that the app now holds a GitHub access token for subsequent Phase 4 slices.

validation.browser_smoke: blocked — sandbox prevented Wrangler from writing its log file and binding 127.0.0.1 for the local worker process

completed_slices.title: Authenticated GitHub repo list — minimal picker after login
summary: Frontend now calls GitHub's GET /user/repos with the OAuth token already stored in Zustand state and maps the response into a minimal readable repo list.
The main menu auto-opens a minimal GitHub repo picker after successful login, while preserving the seeded repo selector for the existing repo-city flow.
Selecting a GitHub repo highlights it in the picker and stores the chosen repo identity in app state for the later ingestion slice.

validation.worker_build: not_run — no worker or shared files changed in this slice
validation.browser_smoke: blocked — sandbox prevented Wrangler from writing its log file and binding 127.0.0.1 for the local worker process

completed_slices.title: Selected GitHub repo metadata ingest — normalize one public repo snapshot
summary: Worker now exposes a read-only GET /api/github/repo-metadata endpoint that accepts repo owner and name query params and defaults to octocat/Hello-World for a real public snapshot.

The worker fetches GitHub REST repo metadata plus language byte totals, then normalizes the response into a RepoModel-compatible snapshot with unknown archetype and empty modules, dependencies, and signals for now.
Shared RepoModel types now support optional normalized provider metadata so stars, forks, topics, branch, and other safe repo facts can flow forward without inventing a one-off payload.

completed_slices.title: Selected GitHub repo metadata ingest trigger — call the worker from the chosen repo picker
summary: The GitHub repo picker now calls the worker's GET /api/github/repo-metadata endpoint with only the selected public repo owner and name when a new repo is chosen.
The trigger is intentionally fire-and-forget for this slice, so repo selection remains non-blocking and no ingest-result UI or city-loading flow was added yet.
Repeated clicks on the already selected repo and non-public repo selections do not trigger ingest, which keeps the MVP from making overeager or ineligible metadata requests.

validation.worker_build: not_run — no worker or shared files changed in this slice
validation.browser_smoke: blocked — sandbox prevented Wrangler from writing its log file and binding 127.0.0.1 for the local worker process

completed_slices.title: Selected GitHub repo metadata snapshot storage — keep the ingest response in frontend state
summary: The frontend now stores the normalized GitHub repo metadata ingest response in volatile Zustand state instead of discarding it after the picker triggers the worker call.

Picker-driven ingest clears stale snapshot data on repo changes, skips duplicate churn for repeated clicks on the already selected repo, and avoids retaining mismatched data for non-public selections.
The stored snapshot uses the shared GitHubRepoMetadataSnapshot contract so later Phase 4 slices can read real repo metadata from app state without adding persistence or routing changes yet.

validation.worker_build: not_run — no worker or shared files changed in this slice
validation.browser_smoke: blocked — sandbox prevented Wrangler from writing its log file and binding 127.0.0.1 for the local worker process

current_slice_recommendation.title: GitHub repo tree module extraction — derive top-level modules in the normalized snapshot
current_slice_recommendation.why: The frontend now retains the selected repo's normalized metadata snapshot in volatile state, but the snapshot still carries empty modules and cannot yet drive district generation for a real repo. The next bounded Phase 4 slice is to fetch a shallow default-branch tree or top-level contents during ingest and derive a small RepoModel module layer so later city-generation work has real structure to consume.
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/github/repo-metadata returns a normalized RepoModel snapshot with a bounded top-level modules array for common public repos
The extracted modules stay metadata-first and avoid deep per-file analysis or source persistence
Existing frontend ingest and selected-repo state continue to accept the response without UI rewrites
cd worker && npm run build passes
If shared contracts change, cd frontend && npm run build passes

open_questions: Should GitHub integration use OAuth first or a GitHub App first?
How much private repo content should ever be inspected?
Will the first generated city be top-down DOM/SVG or lightweight canvas/R3F?
Should the existing deployed game be preserved in a separate mode while the pivot is built?
known_risks: Trying to understand arbitrary code too deeply too early will stall delivery.
Overly literal graph rendering will produce unreadable city layouts.
GitHub trust/privacy concerns can kill adoption if handled casually.
A huge frontend rewrite without a data-model foundation will create theme without product.

global_cycle_rule: Every post-Phase-0 cycle must change at least one non-doc file.
Every post-Phase-0 cycle must run at least one validation command.
If UI flow changes, browser smoke should be run unless explicitly blocked.
phase_1: worker build passes if backend/shared contracts change
frontend build passes if UI/data contracts change
